top_categories.workout_context: 71ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Gran Fondo Panama, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Panama. Train it until it feels boring.
78 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Panama's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
5,512ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Gran Fondo Panama's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 78 miles.
5,512ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Base fitness for Gran Fondo Panama. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 78 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,512ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Panama’s 78 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and G Spot to prepare you for 78 miles of mixed terrain in Panama City, Panama.
